basis: the current method should be compressed into a reusable template chain
downstream steps need variable mapping and handoff rules
the current flow needs explicit shared-context and execution coordination
template outputs will be consumed by multiple bounded execution units

reasons: The round is no longer only solving one instance; it is packaging a reusable method chain.
Template handoff structure and variable mapping must become explicit.
Execution paths, shared files, and reference_context boundaries must be explicit.
The template chain alone does not tell workers who writes what or how writeback is audited.

template_mode.why: template compression depends on already-stable upstream outputs

orchestration_and_memory_mode.why: execution topology and reference_context boundaries depend on stable branch structure

mode_precedence.precedence_basis: template mode defines reusable method semantics and variable handoffs
mode_precedence.note: Orchestration consumes the template outputs but should not rewrite the template chain's semantic intent.

mode_precedence.precedence_basis: orchestration defines execution topology, write scopes, and writeback policy for the current round
mode_precedence.note: Template mode does not own execution authority.

cross_mode_handoffs.handoff_purpose: Provide stable template steps, variable handoffs, and dependencies before execution topology is assigned.
cross_mode_handoffs.conflict_if_missing: Orchestration may build worker packets with no stable upstream method boundary.

cross_mode_handoffs.handoff_purpose: Bind execution_units, worker scopes, and reference_context_refs into one executable coordination surface.
cross_mode_handoffs.conflict_if_missing: Execution identity may fall back to informal agent language instead of explicit unit contracts.

cross_mode_handoffs.handoff_purpose: Preserve observed writebacks and merge outcomes that later template refinement may reuse.
cross_mode_handoffs.conflict_if_missing: Template evolution loses concrete evidence about which handoffs actually worked.

cross_mode_handoffs.handoff_purpose: Expose which paths were pruned or suspended and which replacement summaries should align with later memory_edit_events.
cross_mode_handoffs.conflict_if_missing: Writeback audit may record memory edits without a stable pruning and replacement boundary.

authority_boundaries.subject: execution unit identity, worker scope, and reference_context handling

interaction_notes.active_when: the round both packages a reusable method chain and coordinates bounded execution units
interaction_notes.note: Keep template semantics upstream and execution policy downstream.

interaction_notes.active_when: shared files are used as coordination channels
interaction_notes.note: shared-context-map.json should be treated as an orchestration-owned planning source; writeback-ledger.json should be treated as an execution evidence sink.

interaction_notes.active_when: execution identity is being assigned to bounded workers
interaction_notes.note: Prefer execution_units, worker_scopes, and reference_context_refs over informal child-agent identity language.

interaction_notes.active_when: paths are pruned, suspended, or replaced during orchestration-heavy rounds
interaction_notes.note: Prefer memory_pruning, memory_edit_events, and replacement_summaries over generic history-editing tool language.
